title: Redis — Caching and Advanced Patterns
learningObjectives: Explain **Redis caching** with production trade-offs, SLO thinking, and failure modes
Implement small **Java** snippets that anchor interview whiteboard discussions
Connect **observability** and **capacity** signals to real operational decisions
Identify common **pitfalls** that cause incidents or cost surprises at scale
Compare alternatives using **constraints** (latency, cost, team skills, compliance)
Present answers with **structure**: problem, options, decision, verification

sections.title: Why Redis — Caching and Advanced Patterns matters
sections.content: **Redis** is the **fast** **mutable** tier—misused it becomes **data** **loss**, **stale** reads, or **outage** **multipliers**. Senior answers include **TTL**, **stampede** control, and **memory** limits.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Redis — Caching and Advanced Patterns
sections.content:
### Day 71 — **Redis**

### 1. Patterns
**Cache-aside**, **write-through**, **rate** limiting.

### 2. Consistency
**TTL**, **invalidation**, **eventual** **staleness** UX.

### 3. Data structures
**Hash**, **ZSET**, **HyperLogLog** use cases.

### 4. Ops
**Persistence** modes, **memory** pressure, **slowlog**.

### 5. Failure
Origin overload on mass miss.

### 6. Story
Product **detail** cache—**jittered** TTL + **singleflight** style lock to stop stampede.

sections.title: Basic — Redis roles
sections.output:
Redis: in-memory data structure server
cache: faster reads; not source of truth alone for money

sections.title: Intermediate — TTL jitter toy
Advanced — Stampede guard phrase
sections.output: stampede: mutex per key or request coalescing to origin

sections.title: Cache-aside sequence
sections.description: App reads cache then DB on miss.
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 71 — Cache-aside
App -> Cache : get
Cache --> App : miss
App -> DB : load
App -> Cache : set TTL
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Redis — Caching and Advanced Patterns (Day 71 assignment)
sections.problem:
Print **exactly** two lines:
1. Implemented: Redis - Caching & Advanced Patterns
2. Focus: Cache stampede prevention design.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is **cache** **stampede**?
conceptual.answer: Many requests miss together and thunder on origin—needs coordination or probabilistic early refresh. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Redis caching**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Many requests miss together and thunder on origin—needs coordination or probabilistic early refresh. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Many requests miss together and thunder on origin—needs coordination or probabilistic earl Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

conceptual.question: **TTL** **jitter** why?
conceptual.answer: Avoid synchronized expiry waves across keys. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Redis caching**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Avoid synchronized expiry waves across keys. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Avoid synchronized expiry waves across keys. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

conceptual.question: **Cache-aside** vs **read-through**?
conceptual.answer: Who loads on miss—app code vs integrated layer. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Pattern | Recall |
|---|---|
| Cache-aside | App manages load |
| TTL jitter | Avoid thunder |
| Singleflight | One origin load |
| NX EX | Lock + expire |
| Rate limit | INCR windows |
| Streams | Log-like consume |
| Persistence | RDB/AOF trade |
| Hot key | Shard or replica read |
| Memory | maxmemory policy |
| Slowlog | Find bad commands |
